Hey everybody, welcome to the module on script building. What I refer to is the key to delivering the perfect sale every time. Let me start this module by saying that scripts are one of the most misunderstood parts of all selling. To the point where many sales people think that using them can in some way be unethical or lacks integrity or make them sound wouldn't actually reduce their closing rate. But the reality happens to be the exact opposite. You see when you're using scripts properly, which basically means learning how to use a script without sounding like you're reading from a script, it will actually increase your closing rate dramatically and help you get massive referrals. So let me start first by telling you exactly what a script is. Now very simply, all a script is is the well thought out essence of the perfect sale. And you repeat that, a script is the well thought out essence of the perfect sale. Let me explain what I mean by that. Now imagine if you could go back in time and review 15 of your best sales presentations and ask yourself, when did I sound the smoothest? When was I most right on target? When was I so elegant that even when I had three or four objections thrown at me, the perfect answers came tumbling off my lips and ended up closing the sale anyway. Well what your script represents is all those perfect sales wrapped into one condensed, absolutely perfect version that you can then commit to memory so you can sound your best at all times. Essentially, it's all your best lines using exactly the right order for a perfect straight line presentation. Meaning, it allows you to establish instant massive rapport on both a conscious and an unconscious level while maintaining complete control of the sale the entire time and always sound like you know exactly what you're talking about even when you don't. And in terms of objections, it allows you to answer them in a way that doesn't take you off the straight line to Pluto or your anus. But anyway, there are also some other major benefits here from a management perspective. As much as their ability to systemize their sales force by having the entire sales force saying pretty much the same thing to all its customers regardless of what region they're in or whether they're inside salespeople or outside salespeople in the field, this type of uniformity is crucial when it comes to growing and expanding a company. Not to mention the potential regulatory hassles it saves you when in the absence of script salesmen start going wildly off the straight line and start making stuff up inevitably. Now sometimes salesmen who do that are actually bad in tension. They're saying things for the wrong reasons but very often it's not that. The salesman didn't want to lie or be deceitful. They just simply ran out of accurate, intelligent things to say. And then in one heat of the moment they basically just started making some stuff up out of fear of losing the sale and it wasn't quite ethical or accurate. So a good script prevents that. And what a good script also does is addresses each of the five core elements of the straight line and it addresses them in exactly the right order or syntax as the phrase goes. And it allows you to create both an airtight logical case and an airtight emotional case for each of the three tens and then to effortlessly answer all of your clients' objections while looping around again and again and again to build that 100% absolute certainty and then ultimately decrease their action threshold and if necessary raise their pain flesh old and then calmly ask for the order. It's that simple. Thank you. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye. Bye.
